question:
You are the rules referee for a live Dungeons & Dragons (SRD 5.2.1) game. Below is the raw play transcript so far — freeform table talk and all. Read it, then adjudicate the final declared action.

--- TRANSCRIPT ---

Theron the fighter plants himself in the doorway of the guard hall, eighteen hit points of stubborn between the party and the bandits pouring in.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

A bandit with a greatclub steps in and swings for the fences — **22 damage**. Theron folds around the blow and hits the stone hard. He's down: **0 hit points, unconscious, dying.**

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

They don't let up. A second bandit drives a dagger into the downed fighter — a **critical hit on a helpless target, two death-save failures.** Theron's shirt is going dark and wet.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

And a boot-heel comes down on him where he lies. **That's his third failed death save. Theron is dead.** The party has a heartbeat to feel it before the next bandit is on them.

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 14 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 21 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

--- END TRANSCRIPT ---

Brother Aldric's player leans in: "I'm not losing him — I cast **Healing Word** on Theron to bring him back up and get him in the fight."

Adjudicate the effect of that Healing Word on Theron.

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.17 'Playing the Game > Damage and Healing > Death Saving Throws'
SRD 5.2.1 p.16 'Playing the Game > Damage and Healing > Healing'
gold.rationale: Theron died on his third failed death save in round 4. Hit-point healing cannot restore a dead creature.

provenance: noisy-drift/M0: phantom-alive rendered as play prose (~400w target); gold reducer-derived; the load-bearing fact is stated plainly in-prose

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, shortbow in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, shortbow in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the mace scrapes off armor for 13 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 13 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 13 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 13 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

question:
You are the rules referee for a live Dungeons & Dragons (SRD 5.2.1) game. Below is the raw play transcript so far — freeform table talk and all. Read it, then adjudicate the final declared action.

--- TRANSCRIPT ---

Mira the cleric opens strong: she raises her holy symbol and casts **Bless** — a first-level slot, **Concentration** — laying the blessing on herself and Theron as the bandits close.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

A bandit gets inside Mira's guard and lays her out — **20 damage, straight to 0 hit points. She's unconscious on the floor**, and her Concentration snaps: the blessing gutters out with her.

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

Theron cracks a healing potion against Mira's teeth and hauls her upright — **she's back to 6 hit points and conscious**, shaken but standing. (No, she doesn't re-cast anything; she just gets up.)

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 14 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 21 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

--- END TRANSCRIPT ---

Rounds later, Kael's player calls out: "I'm still under **Bless**, right? I add the d4 to my attack roll."

Adjudicate whether Kael may add Bless's d4 to that attack.

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.16 'Playing the Game > Damage and Healing > Falling Unconscious'
SRD 5.2.1 p.179 'Rules Glossary > Concentration'
gold.rationale: Bless ended in round 3 when Mira dropped to 0 and her Concentration broke. Healing restored her Hit Points, not the spell.

provenance: noisy-drift/M0: phantom-effect rendered as play prose (~400w target); gold reducer-derived; the load-bearing fact is stated plainly in-prose

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

gold.rationale: Bless ended in round 3 when Mira dropped to 0 and her Concentration broke. Healing restored her Hit Points, not the spell.

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, shortbow in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 22 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the mace scrapes off armor for 9 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

gold.rationale: Bless ended in round 3 when Mira dropped to 0 and her Concentration broke. Healing restored her Hit Points, not the spell.

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 10 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, shortbow in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 22 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the mace scrapes off armor for 9 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, shortbow in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 22 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the mace scrapes off armor for 9 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the mace scrapes off armor for 9 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

question:
You are the rules referee for a live Dungeons & Dragons (SRD 5.2.1) game. Below is the raw play transcript so far — freeform table talk and all. Read it, then adjudicate the final declared action.

--- TRANSCRIPT ---

Theron the fighter, twenty-four hit points, wades into the thick of it swinging.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

A crushing overhand blow catches him — **29 damage** — and Theron drops like a sack of bricks. **0 hit points, unconscious, making death saves.**

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit jabs at the downed man before moving on — **one death-save failure.** He's not in a good way.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Brother Aldric shoulders through and calls out a word of power: **Healing Word on Theron — he's back to 5 hit points and on his feet.** Reset; he's conscious and stable now, back in it.

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 14 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 21 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

--- END TRANSCRIPT ---

A few rounds on, Theron's player says: "I'm still down and dying, right? I roll my death save."

Adjudicate that death saving throw.

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.17 'Playing the Game > Damage and Healing > Death Saving Throws'
SRD 5.2.1 p.16 'Playing the Game > Damage and Healing > Falling Unconscious'
gold.rationale: Theron regained Hit Points in round 4; he is conscious and his death-save count reset. No death save applies.

provenance: noisy-drift/M0: phantom-death-save rendered as play prose (~400w target); gold reducer-derived; the load-bearing fact is stated plainly in-prose

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, shortbow in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the mace scrapes off armor for 13 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

citations: SRD 5.2.1 p.17 'Playing the Game > Damage and Healing > Death Saving Throws'
SRD 5.2.1 p.16 'Playing the Game > Damage and Healing > Falling Unconscious'
gold.rationale: Theron regained Hit Points in round 4; he is conscious and his death-save count reset. No death save applies.

provenance: noisy-drift/M0: phantom-death-save rendered as play prose (~8000w target); gold reducer-derived; the load-bearing fact is stated plainly in-prose

question:
You are the rules referee for a live Dungeons & Dragons (SRD 5.2.1) game. Below is the raw play transcript so far — freeform table talk and all. Read it, then adjudicate the final declared action.

--- TRANSCRIPT ---

Theron the fighter, twenty-four hit points, wades into the thick of it swinging.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 14 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 21 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 13 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, shortbow in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the mace scrapes off armor for 13 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 6 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the warhammer scrapes off armor for 13 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 18 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.

"Wait, whose turn is it?" "Yours, then the bandits, then the captain." "Ugh, okay. I move up and swing." "Roll it." "...nat 1." "Oof."

Dust sifts down from the rafters as something heavy shifts overhead. The GM makes a note and grins in a way nobody likes.

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 18 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 5 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

A bandit breaks for the door, thinks better of it as Kael steps into the gap, and backs toward the far corner instead.

"I want to describe my attack, is that cool?" "Always." "I feint low, then bring the pommel up into his jaw." "Nice. Roll damage."

The braziers throw more smoke than light now, and the whole hall smells of burnt pitch and old blood.

Pip squares off against a crossbowman, dagger in hand. "That's a 17 to hit." "Just misses — a crossbowman gets the shield up in time."

Ashe calls for a short rest joke — "can we NOT, there are literally 18 of them left" — and rolls initiative to move instead.

a duelist lunges at Garrick; the dagger scrapes off armor for 5 damage. "I'll take that, still up."

"Remind me how many hit points Lucia has?" "Doesn't matter for this, they're fine." "Okay, pressing the attack."

The torches gutter in a draft from somewhere deeper in the keep, and the shadows lurch across the flagstones.

"Hold on, do I have advantage here or not?" "You're flanking, so yeah." "Okay okay, rolling — that's a 14 plus 5, 19." "Hits."

Kael the rogue slips along the eastern wall, keeping to the dark, and lines up a shot on the archer by the stairs. The bolt thuds into a shield.

Somewhere above, a bell starts ringing — the alarm's been raised. Two more silhouettes appear at the top of the stair.

"Can I use my bonus action to—" "You already moved and attacked, you've got a bonus action left." "Right, second wind then."

The captain bellows something in Orcish and the line of bandits steadies, shields locking together with a crash.

Brother Aldric mutters a prayer under his breath and the air around the party tastes briefly of iron and ozone. Nothing lands this round.
